title: ficus aurantiaca
intro: Ficus aurantiaca, also known as the orange barked ficus, is a species of fig tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its distinctive orange-barked trunk. The bark of Ficus aurantiaca has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ficus aurantiaca include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Ficus aurantiaca is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by boiling the bark in water or mixing it with a carrier oil to create a topical application.
